divergences: Desktop wallpaper is a CSS gradient approximation of the photographic capture; exact hue/luminance per-pixel values diverge, especially in the saturated purple/magenta corners.

Window vibrancy (backdrop-filter blur) samples the CSS-gradient backdrop instead of the true photo, so the window's left edge (closest to the brightest wallpaper region) shows a larger per-pixel color delta than the plain right side of the window.

List-row icons are inline-SVG Lucide-style approximations; glyph stroke placement/antialiasing differs slightly from the reference's rendered icon font, producing small local diffs inside icon tiles (e.g. the yellow inbox icon on the selected 'dash' row).
